title: IntestLine: a Shiny-based web application to convert the Swiss-roll like intestinal tissue to a linear structure
author: Yuzeir, Altay
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: To allow the comprehensive histological analysis of the whole intestine in one image, the tissue is often rolled to a spiral before imaging. This “Swiss-roll” technique indeed facilitates robust experimental procedures, but it limits the possibilities to study intestinal anatomy in its natural linear structure. Here, we present IntestLine, a Shiny-based open-source application to convert imaging data of intestinal tissues in spiral shape into a linear structure. The resulting mapping of the imaging result allows better visualization of the whole intestine in both proximal-distal and serosa-luminal axis, and facilitates the observation of location-specific cell types and markers. In summary, IntestLine serves as a useful tool to visualize and characterize intestine in future imaging studies.
steps_raw: To allow the comprehensive histological analysis of the whole intestine in one image, the tissue is often rolled to a spiral before imaging. This “Swiss-roll” technique indeed facilitates robust experimental procedures, but it limits the possibilities to study intestinal anatomy in its natural linear structure. Here, we present IntestLine, a Shiny-based open-source application to convert imaging data of intestinal tissues in spiral shape into a linear structure. The resulting mapping of the imaging result allows better visualization of the whole intestine in both proximal-distal and serosa-luminal axis, and facilitates the observation of location-specific cell types and markers. In summary, IntestLine serves as a useful tool to visualize and characterize intestine in future imaging studies.
